title: NEST-GPU receptor ports and cond_beta computation
core_claims: A receptor id is a connection-carried selector for a postsynaptic input port.
cond_beta computes conductance dynamics on the postsynaptic input side.
The resulting synaptic current enters the neuron's membrane ODE.
The 20-port limit is shared by NEST-GPU multi-port CUDA models such as user_m1/user_m2, aeif_cond_beta_multisynapse, and izhikevich_cond_beta.
Current canonical project semantics use positive weights and encode inhibition through E_rev.
accuracy_constraints: Do not describe receptor as an output-side synapse object.
Do not claim every NEST-GPU built-in model supports arbitrary receptor arrays.
Do not claim STP is currently implemented in the canonical CA1 path.
